entry.title: Combined summary - Hourglass V2 Update
entry.summary: The Hourglass proposal has been updated to address community concerns regarding the potential risks associated with the mass liquidation of P2PK funds within the Bitcoin network. The revised version imposes a limit, restricting the amount of Bitcoin that can be spent from P2PK outputs to a maximum of one Bitcoin per block. This measure aims to enhance the security and stability of the Bitcoin network by preventing market disruptions that could occur due to large, simultaneous liquidations. Detailed information about the technical adjustments and the rationale behind these changes is available in the Hourglass Proposal V2 document, which can be accessed at [Hourglass Proposal V2](https://github.com/cryptoquick/bips/blob/hourglass-v2/bip-hourglass-v2.mediawiki). The initial discussions and community feedback that shaped the evolution of this proposal can be revisited through the [Bitcoin Development Mailing List Discussion](https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoindev/c/zmg3U117aNc/m/lDCMs9j7EAAJ), highlighting the collaborative effort to refine the proposal and address vulnerabilities in Bitcoin's framework. The introduction of mechanisms like Hourglass to protect against mass liquidation events has sparked debate within the Bitcoin community. Critics argue that such measures conflict with Bitcoin's foundational principles by artificially restricting users' ability to spend their coins, potentially compromising the integrity and utility of Bitcoin as a decentralized, peer-to-peer electronic cash system. This perspective stresses the importance of preserving Bitcoin's original vision as an efficient and unrestricted medium of exchange. Despite criticisms, there is recognition that Bitcoin operates under a set of rules enforced by its users, who possess the economic power to protect the system against threats from malicious actors. The revised Hourglass proposal, particularly the V2 version, finds favor among some for its approach to safeguarding against massive liquidation events while acknowledging its limitations, such as the seemingly arbitrary limit of one BTC per block. There is also interest in how Hourglass V2 might complement other proposals aimed at enhancing Bitcoin's security, such as introducing quantum-safe spending options for users unable to migrate UTXOs to quantum-safe scripts before certain deadlines. This ongoing dialogue underscores the community's proactive stance on identifying and implementing measures to strengthen Bitcoin's resilience and adaptability in the face of evolving challenges.
